input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (includes android, ios stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows SDK and WinUI/UWP build support. The easiest path:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required workload components:

```powershell
# From an elevated prompt — adjust path if using BuildTools
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --quiet --norestart
```

## Step 4 — Set Up the Android SDK

The MAUI workload bundles a minimal Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and discover what was installed:

```powershell
# Find where the workload put the Android SDK
dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdkPath"

# Alternatively, check the default location
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = "$env:USERPROFILE\.android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK: $androidSdk"

# List installed platforms & build-tools dynamically
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level if needed (discover latest first)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"
# Then install, e.g.:
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0"
```

## Step 5 — Install OpenJDK (if not bundled)

```powershell
# Check if java is already available (MAUI workload often bundles it)
java -version

# If missing:
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.21

# Set JAVA_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
    "JAVA_HOME",
    (Get-Command java).Source | Split-Path | Split-Path,
    "User"
)
```

## Step 6 — Create and Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold a new MAUI app
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 7 — Run on Each Target

```powershell
# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# List available Android emulators / devices
& "$androidSdk\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds
# Or use a connected device:
& "$androidSdk\platform-tools\adb.exe" devices

# Deploy to Android emulator/device
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 8 — Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

If you're unsure which TFMs are valid for your SDK version:

```powershell
# See what the template generated
Select-String -Path HelloMaui\HelloMaui.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"

# See available Android API levels on your machine
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed |
    Select-String "platforms;android-"

# See available Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
    Select-Object Name
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Use `net9.0-*` TFMs (or `net10.0-*` if you installed .NET 10).
- The `dotnet workload install maui` command pulls Android SDK, build-tools, and emulator images automatically.
- If `sdkmanager.bat` isn't at the `latest` path, run `Get-ChildItem "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools" -Recurse -Filter sdkmanager.bat` to locate it.
- An **elevated (admin) PowerShell** is needed for the VS installer and workload install steps.
